findings.description: Dedup key is recipient address only (`msg.to`), so any second message to the same address — regardless of subject or body — is silently dropped and counted as skipped; the docstring claims to deduplicate 'a duplicate', implying content-identity, but the implementation enforces address-uniqueness for the lifetime of the object.

On any `sendmail` exception the loop aborts mid-flight: `self._pending.clear()` (line 65) is never reached, so the queue retains all original messages including those already successfully sent; a subsequent `flush()` call re-sends the already-delivered messages, producing duplicate delivery for every message that preceded the failure.

`smtplib.SMTP(SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT)` is constructed outside the `try` block; if the constructor raises (server unreachable), no `finally` cleanup runs for that iteration, and the exception propagates immediately, again leaving `_pending` uncleared and producing duplicate sends on retry.

`server.quit()` in `finally` can itself raise (e.g. `SMTPServerDisconnected`) if the server already closed the socket after a `sendmail` error, which would replace the original exception with an unrelated transport error and obscure the true failure cause.

`_seen` is never cleared by `flush()`, so after a successful flush any re-enqueue attempt for a previously-sent recipient address is permanently silently rejected for the lifetime of the `EmailQueue` instance, even though the prior message has already been delivered.

`clear_dedup_cache()` clears `_seen` but not `_pending`; if pending messages exist at call time, re-enqueuing the same addresses succeeds, resulting in duplicate `Message` objects in `_pending` that will both be sent on the next `flush()`.

`_render` hand-rolls RFC 2822 wire format without validating or encoding `msg.to` or `msg.subject`; an address containing a newline (e.g. injected via untrusted input) breaks the header structure — note `MIMEText` is imported but unused. Flagging for completeness; defer header-injection angle to critic-security.

`enqueue` performs no input validation on `msg.to`; an empty string or whitespace-only address is accepted into the queue and will cause `sendmail` to raise at flush time, triggering the partial-failure / duplicate-send defect described at line 57.
